items.title: Bottega Veneta's new CEO and the moves at Gucci Racing
items.summary: Italian trade press ties Kering's leadership reshuffle together: Romain Spitzer (ex-LVMH Beauty) named Bottega Veneta CEO effective September 1, alongside Tancredi Vitale's move from Venezia FC to lead Gucci Racing as VP. Framed as evidence of Kering CEO Luca de Meo recruiting external, cross-house talent (including from rival LVMH) as part of the wider Gucci-focused turnaround push.

items.title: Kering: Romain Spitzer appointed Chief Executive Officer of Bottega Veneta
items.summary: Official Kering press release confirming Romain Spitzer as Bottega Veneta CEO from September 1, reporting to Luca de Meo. Included for context as it anchors the Gucci-adjacent leadership reshuffle theme; de Meo frames the hire as part of unlocking growth potential across Kering's non-Gucci houses while Gucci itself remains the priority turnaround asset.

items.title: Kering stock trades lower as Gucci recovery and cost controls shape outlook
items.summary: German financial-wire coverage noting Kering shares under pressure as investors weigh Gucci's slow creative reset against continued margin pressure; wholesale rationalization and store-network renewal are cited as near-term drags, while Saint Laurent and Bottega Veneta show comparatively stronger momentum. Framed ahead of Kering's H1 2026 results due July 29.

items.title: Caporalato: Milan prosecutors widen fashion labour-exploitation inquiry
items.summary: Update on the Milan Prosecutor's office request for internal supply-chain and audit documentation from roughly ten luxury groups including Gucci, following Carabinieri inspections at subcontractor workshops in Pero and Marnate. No charges or precautionary measures have been issued against any named brand; the inquiry focuses on corporate compliance models rather than direct wrongdoing, but keeps Gucci in ongoing critical Italian press coverage of supply-chain labour practices.

items.title: Gucci and L'Oreal enter 50-year exclusive beauty licence agreement
items.summary: Official joint release confirming Gucci Beauty's licence moves from Coty to L'Oreal a year ahead of schedule (effective mid-2027), an unusually long 50-year term that removes Gucci Beauty from ordinary licence-renewal cycles. Ends a dispute dating to late 2025 when Coty sued Kering and Gucci entities in the UK Commercial Court over the original announcement; still shaping analyst commentary into late July as a rare structural positive within Gucci's broader turnaround narrative.

items.title: Forum thread: Balenciaga or Gucci?
items.summary: Ongoing PurseForum comparison thread where collectors debate Balenciaga versus Gucci on construction quality and resale value; multiple posters say current Gucci quality has slipped since the Tom Ford era ended, though others push back defending recent pieces. Community-tier read on how collector sentiment toward Gucci craftsmanship is holding up during the Demna transition.

items.title: Forum members react to Gucci's Monte Carlo summer campaign
items.summary: Independent fashion-forum roundup of member comments on the Gucci Monte Carlo 2026 campaign; reactions split between approval of a 'finally looking like luxury' direction and criticism that the imagery is 'cheap and dated' and the cast 'doesn't exude opulence.' Retained slightly outside the ideal freshness window because it is the most substantive available community discussion thread specific to the ongoing Monte Carlo campaign arc (chapter two of which published in July).

items.title: Gucci Instagram: Monte Carlo campaign chapter two
items.summary: Official Gucci-owned Instagram reel for the second chapter of the Monte Carlo 2026 summer campaign, shifting from Riviera coastline to hotel interiors and villas, tied to the 60th anniversary of the Flora motif. Flagged per objectivity policy as brand-owned activation content, not independent public opinion; like/comment counts were not extractable from the fetched view and are recorded as null rather than estimated.

items.title: Kering Revenues Declined 6.2% in Q1, with Gucci Still Sluggish
items.summary: Financial-desk recap of Gucci's 14.3 percent reported Q1 revenue decline to 1.35 billion euros, weakest in Western Europe and China even as North America improved 7 percent; remains the reference baseline European trade press uses heading into Kering's July 29 H1 results, against which the L'Oreal beauty deal and de Meo's leadership reshuffle will be judged.

items.title: Gucci, crisi di un modello
items.summary: Italian cultural-economic commentary arguing Kering became structurally over-dependent on Gucci and that a growth-through-distribution model has hit its limits; contends cost-cutting alone cannot fix a desirability-driven luxury business, framing Gucci's troubles as a design and positioning crisis as much as a financial one. A critical-angle opinion piece distinct from routine earnings recaps, still being referenced in Italian commentary this week.
